I think, gentlemen, we need a proper night’s sleep to really let our anger reach its full potential. HILBERT I concur wholeheartedly, Commander. If you require any sentiments beyond the reach of the English language, I am happy to supply alternatives in Russian, Norwegian, German, Swedish, or Afrikaans. EIFFEL Hey, kids, I’ll log it in all six. MINKOWSKI Sounds good. Good night, Eiffel. Doctor. HILBERT Good night all. EIFFEL Good night! The Comms Room door CLOSES. 17. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Oh, hell, speaking of logs... I guess you caught all of that, so you might be able to pick up the effect your twisted experiment had on us. Hint: IT WAS AWFUL. Sorry if things got a bit crazy for a while there, dear listeners, but... well, you see the kind of things we have to deal with. EIFFEL (CONT'D) And I used to complain about my boss at Pizza Hut... Anyway, from the U.S.S. Hephaestus Station, this is Communications Officer Doug Eiffel, signing off. Good night, folks! STOP RECORDING. END EPISODE.

episode_id: Mini Episode - Adventure of a Lifetime
text: WOLF 359 "ADVENTURE OF A LIFETIME" by Zach Valenti, Sarah Shachat, and Gabriel Urbina NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. EIFFEL This is the audio log of Communications Officer Doug Eiffel, on board the U.S.S. Hephaestus Station. And today, we're going on a field trip into the outer rim of our inexplicably massive space station. The Commander made it abundantly clear we should keep to the central hub, which means there're all these rooms in the outer rim that no one's ever been to! Until today. Are you ready to boldly go where no man has... probably... gone before? Not gonna wait for your response because, well, recording. So let's go on the adventure of a lifetime! STATIC. Door OPENS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Alright! Adventure of a - Oh. It's just kind of a room. Nothing in here. Super gray. Whoof. STATIC. Door OPENS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Alright! Adventure of a - huh. It's another empty room. How exciting... STATIC. Door OPENS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Alright, adventure of a - Goddamm- really? Is there anything in any of these rooms? Ugh. ... Huh. Wait a minute. There might be something in that ... Nah. Just a different shade of gray paint on the wall. Well, folks, this whole "adventure of a -" CLANG. The door SHUTS. 2. EIFFEL (CONT'D) What the - was - was that the door? He PULLS at the handle. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Is that the locked door?! ... Uhh. We seem to be having some technical difficulties? But never fear! I'm sure we'll figure this out in no time. I'll give it two hours. Tops. STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) I'm hungry... I didn't have breakfast this morning... It's been five hours... But. Okay. It's okay! It's okay. I'm sure someone noticed I'm missing, and help is on the way. Help is on the... STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) You know what it's like to be in a room with nothing in it? It's been ten hours and I - Oh God. Is that - what was that sound? DISTANT CLANGING. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Were those footsteps? ... Wait. There are no footsteps in space. Ugh. I - I think I'm finally starting to crack up. Yeah, definitely starting to... STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) You know, at first I didn't appreciate this slightly different shade of gray on the wall, but, eighteen hours later? It's really speaking to me. No, like, actually

episode_id: Mini Episode - Adventure of a Lifetime
text: no footsteps in space. Ugh. I - I think I'm finally starting to crack up. Yeah, definitely starting to... STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) You know, at first I didn't appreciate this slightly different shade of gray on the wall, but, eighteen hours later? It's really speaking to me. No, like, actually speaking to me. Like - wait. What's that, gray spot? You want me to kill everyone? You want me to kill everyone with a- STATIC 3. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ohhhkay. That got a little weird. But, my very dear listeners, you'll be glad to hear I am no longer speaking with the gray spot on the wall. Because, to be honest, he's a bad influence. But we've reached an understanding! Mostly that this is where I die because no one's coming for me. He SIGHS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) So... now... in this, my final will and testament, I bequeath all my po- ... Well... uhh... I guess I don't exactly have my poss... My bones! My very valuable bones to this gray spot on the wall in this empty room. Because no one is coming to sa- The door OPENS. HILBERT Eiffel. What are you doing in here? EIFFEL Oh, thank God! Doctor Hilbert, how did you find - wait. How did you open the door?! HILBERT I simply pulled. And door opens. EIFFEL That's exactly what I've been trying to do! HILBERT Did you try... pushing? EIFFEL ... I don't have to answer that. I've been trapped in here for thirty-six hours, you have no idea what that does to a person. HILBERT Eiffel. You have been gone for nineteen minutes. BEAT. 4. EIFFEL How about this: you never mention any of this, ever again, to anyone, and I don't ask you what you're doing on this part of the station? HILBERT Agreed. FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

WOLF 359 "SPACE RAGE" by Zach Valenti, Sarah Shachat, and Gabriel Urbina NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. EIFFEL And now, Doug Eiffel presents, live from the U.S.S. Hephaestus Station, orbiting red dwarf star Wolf 359: "Space Rage", with your host, Badass McAwesome. BADASS Good evening, Sprage Nation! Welcome back to another edition of Space Rage, with me, Badass McAwesome: the only voice left broadcasting the truth, stickin' it to the man, and letting you get heard eight light years away from Earth. Out fight against dictatorship, authoritarianism, pettiness, and bad taste continues, and I’m here as the last angry man with the guts to take a stand. "Badass McAwesome" is - of course - Eiffel doing his best impression of, well. Of Libertarian talk radio. BADASS (CONT'D) Tonight’s topic: censorship. That’s right. We’ll uncover its history, its effects, and why one bitter, angry husk of a person would stoop so low as to violate our God-given American right to free speech! And funny jokes! But this revolution is bigger than me. Wayyy bigger. And that’s why we want you to call in and share your battle scars from our crusade against censorship! So call in to... the comms room. And let your voice be heard! COMMS BUZZER. BADASS (CONT'D) Look at that. We got our first patriot on the line. He FLIPS a switch. BADASS (CONT'D) You're on the air. Let freedom ring, caller! 2. EIFFEL (speaking through a voice box) Thanks, Badass! Love the show, long time first time! BADASS Glad to have ya. What's your name, soldier? Whatcha dealing with? EIFFEL My name’s Doug, and my boss is being really unreasonable and censoring my freedom of expression! BADASS We've all been there. We've all been there, son. What are they tryin' to keep you from gettin' out? EIFFEL So, first thing’s first: I’m stuck in this craptastic job, listening to static for fourteen hours a day, searching for alien life... BADASS We've all certainly been there. EIFFEL But then, get this! I’m the kind of guy that takes a certain amount of pride in my work. I - I shouldn’t even call it my work - my craft. Really, my art. That’s why, for the outbound calls, I try to go above and beyond. To bring a little... personal touch! A little je ne sais quoi. If that means what I think it

in my work. I - I shouldn’t even call it my work - my craft. Really, my art. That’s why, for the outbound calls, I try to go above and beyond. To bring a little... personal touch! A little je ne sais quoi. If that means what I think it means. BADASS Yessir, Monsieur. EIFFEL And yeah, sure, there’s like, this script you’re supposed to broadcast for some SETI protocol thingy... BADASS Sounds like the man trying to keep you down, if you ask me. 3. EIFFEL Yeah! Thanks, Badass. That’s why I’ve tried to spice it up. That’s why I’ve been reciting every Simpsons Treehouse of Horror episode from memory. BADASS Just the way Abe Lincoln woulda wanted! EIFFEL But my boss keeps trying to shut me down! She says I’m on a curfew until I "do it right." It's like she hasn't even read the Constitution! BADASS Ah, see, well this is how authoritarianism works! It crushes the soul of the sensitive artiste, makes it so they can’t get donuts after 6 P.M.! But, Doug, you stay strong. You keep fighting that good fight! We're counting on you. Never. Weaken. He FLIPS a switch. BADASS (CONT'D) Alright. Before our next segment, a word from our sponsors: Doctor Hilbert’s Seaweed Coffee! It’s not seaweed, it’s not coffee, it kinda keeps you awake, but it’s the only thing we have! It’s the only thing we have. Alright, now for our next segment, we - COMMS BUZZER. EIFFEL (breaking character) What the - ? He CLEARS HIS THROAT. BADASS Well, that's strange. Seems we have another caller on the line. He FLIPS a switch. 4. BADASS (CONT'D) You're live! Let freedom ring! MINKOWSKI Hi there! First time listener, first time caller. BADASS Uh, well, what can I do for you? MINKOWSKI You can answer a question for me: is there someone in the comms room who shouldn’t be there after curfew? BADASS Well, no, ma’am. No people here, just some ideals. Like truth! And justice! And liberty -! Minkowski forces the door open. BADASS (CONT'D) - And you'll never take me alive! Nothing can stop the movement, nothing can s - CRASH. Glass SHATTERING. All the sounds of a scuffle as Minkowski goes after Eiffel and he tries to wriggle away. EIFFEL (breaking character) Ow ow ow, please stop, please - ow, ow, ow, okay, okay, you're taking me alive! Please,

alive! Nothing can stop the movement, nothing can s - CRASH. Glass SHATTERING. All the sounds of a scuffle as Minkowski goes after Eiffel and he tries to wriggle away. EIFFEL (breaking character) Ow ow ow, please stop, please - ow, ow, ow, okay, okay, you're taking me alive! Please, I'm sorry, just let go of my - ow! (as Badass) That's all the time we have for today! Comin' at you live from the comms room, this has been Space R- (normal voice; fading) - Owww-ww, surrender! Space Surrender! Remember! Always weaken! FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

WOLF 359 "STEP ONE" by Zach Valenti, Sarah Shachat, and Gabriel Urbina NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. EIFFEL This is the audio log of Doug Eiffel, Communications Officer aboard the U.S.S. Hephaestus Station, orbiting red dwarf star Wolf 359! I’m here, just outside one of our primary airlocks, and we’re about to go outside. Why are we going outside? Well, funny story! See, a couple of days ago it came to my attention that our stick-in-the-mud, insufferable Commander keeps... a diary! A diary with all kinds of intense security protocols built into the lock. So I kinda grabbed it and was taking it by the workshop and, well, yadda yadda yadda... it’s now floating in outer space. I have about... two hours before she notices it’s gone? And kills me? So, if this tape shows up in my murder trial, you’ll know why I made this recording. ... But that won’t happen! Because, you guys? Two hours is so much time! It’s like, an acre of time. It’s - no, it’s an Empire State Building amount of - nope, nopenopenope, sorry, focus, focus, actually do the thing. Eiffel SIGHS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay. So, step one: assemble the spacesuit. I’ve never actually worn one of these things before, but they can’t be that hard to put together, right? He examines the spacesuit. EIFFEL (CONT'D) I mean, the legs go here, and the arms go here, and then this hole is... Wow. What is this hole for? Okay, maybe the head goes... maybe this is where the arms... No, you know what, guys? I think this is one of those "let's try it out" kind of problems. What's the worst that could happen? 2. STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (panting) Okay. Okay. So, slight complications: turns out that there are a lot of parts that are stretchy, but also a lot of parts that aren't so stretchy - and jagged! Why are there so many jagged parts? What possible purpose could there be for jagged parts on the inside of a - Oh, actually... Wow. I guess I'm not sure this is even really the inside, but... that shouldn't matter, right? I mean, if something's waterproof on one side, it's waterproof on the other... riiight? ... Alright, hang on a sec. I think I got this. STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) I don't got this! I repeat: I totally don't got

sure this is even really the inside, but... that shouldn't matter, right? I mean, if something's waterproof on one side, it's waterproof on the other... riiight? ... Alright, hang on a sec. I think I got this. STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) I don't got this! I repeat: I totally don't got this! Ow ow oww, I can't feel my leg, get it off get it off get it off get it off get it ooffff!!! STATIC EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay. Hang on a second. I think I just need to take a moment and think this through. STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) No, you know what? I think I'm overthinking this! I think I just need to dive right in. STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ohhhh-kay. Okay, I think I have thought about this exactly the right amount. STATIC. 3. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ah-ha! And now, if I'm not mistaken, all I have to do is turn this knob, and that should do - CLICK. WHIR. A sequence of UNPLEASANT MECHANICAL SOUNDS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ow ow owww! Turn it off! Why do we even have that knob?! STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (panting) Victory! I've done it. I've finally managed to get every single part of the spacesuit off. Wow. Who knew this could be so complicated?! So... maybe I should get a manual and try again, but it's okay. I've still totally got... - fourteen minutes?! I think it may be time for Plan B. STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) I think it may be time for Plan C! STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) I think it may be time to... panic because I don't have any more plans! No. Wait. I have the perfect plan! STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Never thought I'd be grateful for the extra journals in the storage room! Okay: He starts WRITING in the blank journal. EIFFEL (CONT'D) "Day one hundred and fourteen." ... Uh... "Eiffel was great! I really appreciate him! I need to lighten up, like, a lot. Okay, bye!" He KISSES the tips of his fingers. 4. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Seamless! What could possibly go - STATIC. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (in pain) Ahh... So many things went wrong. She somehow saw through my forgery. My face hurts. Lots of things hurt. And the worst part is... as punishment, I’ve been assigned a grueling thirty-day work detail. Outside the station. In the spacesuit. He WHIMPERS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Whyyy-yy...? FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

wrong. She somehow saw through my forgery. My face hurts. Lots of things hurt. And the worst part is... as punishment, I’ve been assigned a grueling thirty-day work detail. Outside the station. In the spacesuit. He WHIMPERS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Whyyy-yy...? FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

episode_id: Mission Mishaps - A Little Night Music
text: WOLF 359 "A LITTLE NIGHT MUSIC" by Gabriel Urbina NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. Mission Mishaps, Entry One. Hephaestus Formal Complaint #ARB062. Day 510. STATIC. CLICK. EIFFEL Okay... I think that oughta... He TAPS the mic. FEEDBACK. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ah-ha! See, Hera? I told you I could get the PA system working again. (beat) I did too! (beat) Well, the point is it's working now, okay? And right in the nick of time, too. (clears throat) Good evening, Hephaestus! Welcome to the Doug Eiffel Motivational Boost Hour! Tonight's episode is brought to you by: not fully reading your contract before you sign it, not realizing what some of the fine print is saying, and getting stuck with some jobs that really push the boundaries of what a Communications Officer is supposed to do. (coughs) Our show's also brought to you by: The See, Commander, I'm Doing the Thing, Get Off My Case Already Please and Thank You Foundation. But, uh, in any case, this is supposed to be a... I got this written down somewhere. Hold on a sec... Eiffel FLIPS through some papers. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Here we go. It's a quarterly... (snorts) (MORE) 2. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Quarterly? That's cute. A quarterly station-wide broadcast to lift morale and boost the crew’s spirits. Well, that sounds super... Yeah. I mean, we got the station, and the broadcast, so the morale should just be real... boostable. And we’ll do that by... umm... hm. Eiffel POPS his lips. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Well, uh. Hm. ... Hey, Hera? Hera? You got any ideas for this morale booster-majig? (beat) What do you mean you’re not...? No, I know it's my job. I’m just asking if you have any suggest-... (grumble) Okay, okay, fine! I’ll go figure something out. (to himself) Jesus Christ, does anyone on this station ever think about anyone other than themselves? (clears throat) Anyway. As I was saying... (beat) Yeah, nope. I’ve got nothing. (beat) Well, I don’t! What the hell am I supposed to do to make us feel better about being stuck up here? Tell knock-knock jokes? Host a radio luau? Rank the musical transmissions we’ve picked up from deep space? He SNAPS his fingers. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (there's an idea) Actually... SCENE TRANSITION. Beethoven's Für Elise plays over the next segment. 3. EIFFEL (snooty, pretentious) Good evening, dear listeners, and welcome to the Hephaestus Sonic Artistry

episode_id: Mission Mishaps - A Little Night Music
text: jokes? Host a radio luau? Rank the musical transmissions we’ve picked up from deep space? He SNAPS his fingers. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (there's an idea) Actually... SCENE TRANSITION. Beethoven's Für Elise plays over the next segment. 3. EIFFEL (snooty, pretentious) Good evening, dear listeners, and welcome to the Hephaestus Sonic Artistry Appreciation Program. I’m your host, Doctor Doctor Professor Doctor Douglas F. Eiffel. The third. Esquire. I am here with my many, maaany PhDs to treat you to a sensory journey of the auditive variety through symphonizingly mesmerous luxuries that transcend past the jejune qualities of their incipience to... uh... (normal voice) Music, okay? We're gonna talk about pretty music. Cool? (snooty) It's going to be so cultured. And musical. Hooray! The topic du jour - and if I may say so, I think you’ll find it so apropos for this jour that it will border on the par excellence - is the best, mysterious, unexplained classical pieces of music to help you grit your teeth, (in fact gritting his teeth; voice slipping) ignore that sinking feeling in your stomach that you’re going to die horribly, and crawl your way to the end of another miserable... (snooty voice) ... jour. It’s going to be amazing! Let’s get started. For our first piece, we have a work of absolute genius. It is outstanding. It is beautiful. It... has a name, I’m sure, but who the hell knows what it is? But it’s - you know - the one! With the music. And the piano. (normal voice) Ugh, how the hell did it go? It’s like, uh... Ba bupbupba bupbupba bupbupbalelalaa daa... You know that one? No? I'm sure you do, just listen to this: He tries to sing it again. We can hear Franz Schubert's Fantasy in C playing over his voice. 4. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Yeah?! ... No? Oh, come on, just listen closely! I'm sure you'll get it. And again. The music grows louder over him. EIFFEL (CONT'D) - de da da - Here it comes! - Laaa de da daaa! He stops singing. The music keeps playing. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ahh, yes. The way the piano notes dance around the… other piano notes. It’s just... mwah! Extraordinary year, seven... eighteen... nineteen hundred and... (trails off; mumbles) ... Absolutely extraordinary! It’s a fantastic piece for when you’re trying to start your day off with the right... piano-y... energy. The music FADES OUT.

episode_id: Mission Mishaps - A Little Night Music
text: yes. The way the piano notes dance around the… other piano notes. It’s just... mwah! Extraordinary year, seven... eighteen... nineteen hundred and... (trails off; mumbles) ... Absolutely extraordinary! It’s a fantastic piece for when you’re trying to start your day off with the right... piano-y... energy. The music FADES OUT. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Of course, sometimes you need something a bit more... (macho) Rugged. Somethin' robust. Somethin' that feels like it’s got chest hair on its revolver, and doesn’t care what the sheriff tells it to do. When you’re in that mode, you don’t mess around with wimpy music. You go straight for the... uhh. Bumpy, kinda cowboy-esque classical thing, with... uhh, I think it's a horn? ... It's a working title, okay? (clears throat) You know how this one goes, right? It's like, uh... badabum badabum badabumbumbum, badabum badabum badabumbumbum, badabum badabum badabumbumbum, badabaaa - dabumbumbum... Gioachino Rossini's William Tell Overture plays over his singing. 5. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Oh, yeah. That’s what I’m talking about. That’s the sound of adventure rushing towards you. Of the open field... uh, the open space waiting to be discovered. Conquered. This is the music of power. Of confidence. Of manli- But that last word is cut off by a loud CLANK as he STUBS HIS TOE on the door. EIFFEL (CONT'D) OW!! Ohhh, ow ow ow, I stubbed my toe! Ooooh, gahhh, stupid door! Stupid foot! Ahh, stupid toe! Mmm... mmhm. Okay. Okay, I'm alright. Keep it together. (sniffles) Um, anyway. Neeext! What's next?! (beat) Ohhh yes. Yes, yes, of course. Next, a piece for those days when the clouds gather, and you find some dark thoughts interfering with your work. It has a name. I'm sure it's really boring, so I made it better. Ladies and gentlemen... may I present to you: The Mind Eraser. Pachelbel's Canon in D Major starts to play. Eiffel sings along to it: Buh... buh... buh... buhh... EIFFEL (CONT'D) Now, you might be going: (nerd voice) "Uh, Professor? P-p-p-professor Eiffel, sir? W-why do you call this the-the-the Mind Eraser?" (normal voice) Well, friend, let me tell you: this song is a mutant. And its superpower is being the catchiest thing in the universe. Once you start listening to this song, it. Never. Leaves. And it kicks out anything else that was scheduled to happen in your brain. Having some concerns about how safe those mandatory medical tests are,

episode_id: Mission Mishaps - A Little Night Music
text: song is a mutant. And its superpower is being the catchiest thing in the universe. Once you start listening to this song, it. Never. Leaves. And it kicks out anything else that was scheduled to happen in your brain. Having some concerns about how safe those mandatory medical tests are, because that equipment doesn't look all that - NOPE! That's cancelled! (MORE) 6. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Instead, we're just gonna go: dum da de dum da da de da da dum dum dum da de dum da de dadadade dum dum. ... Try it out! It works with anything. Are you afraid that the Plant Monster's gonna find you in the middle of the night and eat your brain while you sleep? Not anymore you're not! Now all you're thinking is: dum da de dum da da de da da dum dum. What's that you say? You're getting worried that you'll never make it back to Earth? Never get to see your family? Or your friends? Or any of the three good Arnold Schwarzenegger movies ever again? Well, that's an interesting thought! But have you considered: dum da de dum da da de da da dum dum! No problem's too big or small for the Mind Eraser! Scared you're gonna die? Try a bit of the ol' dum da de dum da da de da da dum dum! Feeling a little bit bored? No sweat. We'll fix you up a nice, tall glass of the dum da de dum da da de da da dum dum. (increasing frustration) Feel like your life doesn't mean anything anymore because you're stuck on a space station, trying to do a job you don't understand, for a mission that nobody is willing to explain, with people who blame you for everything that goes wrong, for bosses you're pretty sure are at least kinda evil, not that you can do anything about it because they own you, and every day is just the same thing over and over and over again, only maybe each day gets a little bit more... boring! Or a little bit more painful! Or a little bit more scary! Well, that's okay! You know why?! Because the only thing you'll be thinking is (losing it) dum da de dum da dadadedadedada da da dum da da dum dadada dedadadada da da dum da da dum dadadadadadada de da dum- RECORD SCRATCH. 7.

episode_id: Mission Mishaps - A Little Night Music
text: painful! Or a little bit more scary! Well, that's okay! You know why?! Because the only thing you'll be thinking is (losing it) dum da de dum da dadadedadedada da da dum da da dum dadada dedadadada da da dum da da dum dadadadadadada de da dum- RECORD SCRATCH. 7. He takes a couple of DEEP BREATHS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (reining it in) Okay. That kind of went to a place there. Uhm... (calling outside the room) Hey, Commander? How's that? Was that enough morale boosting? ... What? What d'you mean you put it on mute?! ... After the first two minutes?! ... And turning it off actually raised everyone's morale? (dejected) Oh... Yeah, I guess that does count... Okay... I'll... go back to work then... (back to mic) Well, I uh... guess that's all the time we have tonight, folks! I'll, uh... see you all next quarter. Eiffel SHUTS OFF the PA system. We can hear him TYPING. After a moment, he starts to whistle while he works: the recognizable tune of The Mind Eraser. FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

WOLF 359 "COLD TURKEY" by Scotty Shoemaker NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. Mission Mishaps, Entry Six. Hephaestus Log TRK536. Day 553. INT. U.S.S. HEPHAESTUS STATION - COMMS ROOM EIFFEL This is the audio log of Communications Officer Doug Eiffel. I'm coming to you, dear listeners, from the Comms Room of the U.S.S. Hephaestus, and today... is day five hundred and fifty-three! No, you heard right. Five hundred and fifty-three. Some might call it the best day of the year. Some might call it the most awesome day of the year. Some might call it the most delicious day of the year. But well, yours truly, being a simple down-to-earth soul, I just call it... THANKS-FRICKIN'-GIVING!!! That's right! Tonight, one night only: Juicy turkey slathered in gravy. Stuffing with a steaming side of mashed potatoes. Pecan pie and lembas bread- COMMS BUZZER. MINKOWSKI (over comms) Eiffel, I need - (coughs) - I need a favor. EIFFEL Yeah? MINKOWSKI I know I said that I'd cook dinner tonight, but... EIFFEL Yeah? MINKOWSKI ... but I can't. I think I picked up a cold. 2. EIFFEL From where? How? We're in outer space! MINKOWSKI Really? Really? You, Doug Eiffel, want to go down that road? EIFFEL Yeahh, I didn't think that one th- MINKOWSKI Mr. "No, Really, My Throat Feels Soo Scratchy, I Think I Need Another Sick Day, Commander" - EIFFEL Okay, fine! Nevermind, then. MINKOWSKI Good. (coughs) Would you mind taking over dinner duties? EIFFEL Uh, wait a minute, I - I thought I was banned from the kitchen after the whole Sriracha incident...? MINKOWSKI Would you just do it? EIFFEL Okay, okay! ... But, as amazing as I am at eating Thanksgiving dinner, I don't actually know anything about making Thanksgiving- MINKOWSKI I'm sure you'll figure something out. I'll see you in the Mess at 1900. EIFFEL Wait, but-! COMMS BUZZER. Eiffel SIGHS. 3. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay. No need to panic. I mean, as long as there's turkey, everything should be fine. And I mean, it's just cooking. How hard could it be? A little heat, a little... salt? Psshh. I got this. On that encouraging note, we - CUT TO: INT. U.S.S. HEPHAESTUS STATION - STOREROOM - LATER We hear the door OPEN. EIFFEL Alrighty. I've put off dinner prep long enough. Time to get the turkey out of cold storage. Let's see... He

heat, a little... salt? Psshh. I got this. On that encouraging note, we - CUT TO: INT. U.S.S. HEPHAESTUS STATION - STOREROOM - LATER We hear the door OPEN. EIFFEL Alrighty. I've put off dinner prep long enough. Time to get the turkey out of cold storage. Let's see... He POPS open a freezer and looks inside. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Turkey, turkey, where's the - Okay, must be in the second one. He opens the second freezer and rummages around. EIFFEL (CONT'D) O...kay. What happened to the -? We definitely had some turkey, so where did it...? He keeps searching through the freezer. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay. Okay. No need to panic. Well, I mean, some need to panic, I do need to put together a Thanksgiving dinner and suddenly there's no turkey. (nervous laughter) And, well, what is Turkey Day without the turkey? It's nothing! Just - just Day! And we've had way too many of those up here. Come on, there has to be something in here. He keeps searching with increasing desperation, throwing things aside as he goes: 4. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (muttering) Freeze dried plant proteins... Spam... Spam... baked beans... Spam... dehydrated meat supplement... gruel... unidentified protein sludge... (breathes in through his teeth) Well, friends. It looks like the closest thing we have to turkey is... Spam. Cans of Spam. Four cans of chunky, bouncy Spam. Eiffel GROANS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Of course, some might say: (snooty voice) "Oh, that'll do! That's close enough." (normal voice) And to them I say: Ding ding! Shame! I mean, even Hilbert, freedom-hating foreigner that he is, knows the difference between the true taste of American turkey and... Wait a minute. Hilbert! Transitional WHIP. CUT TO: INT. U.S.S. HEPHAESTUS STATION - HILBERT'S LAB HILBERT Why on Earth would I help with this madness? EIFFEL Because this is important! It's an honest-to-God emergency! HILBERT And this "emergency" you speak of, it is that - Eiffel dumps the cans of Spam onto Hilbert's workstation. EIFFEL I need you to work your voodoo magic on this Spam to make it taste like turkey. 5. Hilbert stares at him, then - HILBERT One moment, Eiffel. CLICK. Hilbert turns on a recording device. HILBERT (CONT'D) Chemical trial log RH45: compound I was exposed to may have psychoactive properties after all. Will study further. CLICK. Hilbert SIGHS. HILBERT (CONT'D) Eiffel, you can not be - EIFFEL Oh, I can be!

him, then - HILBERT One moment, Eiffel. CLICK. Hilbert turns on a recording device. HILBERT (CONT'D) Chemical trial log RH45: compound I was exposed to may have psychoactive properties after all. Will study further. CLICK. Hilbert SIGHS. HILBERT (CONT'D) Eiffel, you can not be - EIFFEL Oh, I can be! I'm totally serious, Doc. Do it in the spirit of Thanksgiving! HILBERT What? Thanks... is that today? EIFFEL Uh-duh! HILBERT Ugh, I do not understand this giving of thanks. How does one give thanks? EIFFEL That's not - HILBERT Can I give indifference? I have plenty of that. EIFFEL We can give thanks to turkey. HILBERT You give the turkey feeling? EIFFEL No! We're - thankful there is turkey. But the giving of thanks is to the people around you and - and the world in general! 6. BEAT. EIFFEL (CONT'D) ... Just turn this Spam into turkey, okay?! HILBERT No, Eiffel. EIFFEL Pleeaase? HILBERT No. EIFFEL Come on! HILBERT Oh, well, if I have to "come on"... EIFFEL Really?! HILBERT NO. EIFFEL Ohhh... (light bulb) ... Oh! I get it. I see. I see. Y'know, Doc, you could just say it. HILBERT Say... it...? EIFFEL Totally! There's no shame in it. HILBERT Shame...? EIFFEL Yeah, shame! Y'know, 'cause you're not good enough? Didn't pay attention in Transfiguration class? I bet Potions was more your thing anyw- HILBERT Enough, Eiffel! (more to himself) (MORE) 7. HILBERT (CONT'D) Success is highly improbable even if theoretically could use compound to trick taste buds to - EIFFEL Perfect! That's the stuff. Ooh, and speaking of stuff, how 'bout some stuffing? HILBERT You want me to stuff... what? EIFFEL No! Stuffing. The food. HILBERT You want me to stuff the food? Into what? Is Spam, Eiffel. Already in can. Very condensed state. EIFFEL No! I - I want you to - while you're working your turkey magic - also make stuffing. Stuffing is a food. Made out of bread. That is cooked inside the turkey. HILBERT Once again, Eiffel: It. Is. Spam. Even if you brought me bread, which you did not, I do not think either of us could conceal bread inside cans of Spam. EIFFEL No, you make it separately! Ah, forget it. Okay. How 'bout some pecan pie? Can you do that? BEAT. Hilbert's expression changes. Intensifies. HILBERT Pecan pie? EIFFEL Yeah! HILBERT I... like pecan pie. EIFFEL ... You do?

not think either of us could conceal bread inside cans of Spam. EIFFEL No, you make it separately! Ah, forget it. Okay. How 'bout some pecan pie? Can you do that? BEAT. Hilbert's expression changes. Intensifies. HILBERT Pecan pie? EIFFEL Yeah! HILBERT I... like pecan pie. EIFFEL ... You do? 8. HILBERT Once had pecan pie at Lula Jane's Bakery in Waco, Texas. Ordered six full pies. Ate nothing else all week. EIFFEL Why were you in Waco...? HILBERT Hmm. I may have been over-hasty, Eiffel. If this Thanksgiving includes pecan pie, maybe is not so terrible. EIFFEL Alright! See, Doc? You're starting to get it. HILBERT Very well, Eiffel. Let us do it. You and I together, we shall cook Thanksgiving dinner! EIFFEL We shall? HILBERT And it will be the greatest Thanksgiving dinner ever made! EIFFEL Yeah! It will! HILBERT Where do we get started? EIFFEL Alright. Step one - At that moment, the door OPENS. MINKOWSKI Eiffel? Hilbert? What the heck are you two doing? Dinner's ready. Your turkey's going to get cold. EIFFEL Commander? I was just - Wait, what?! MINKOWSKI Thanksgiving dinner. You do remember Thanksgiving, right? (MORE) 9. MINKOWSKI (CONT'D) Hera found some extra turkey in the reserve freezer yesterday. HILBERT She did? MINKOWSKI Oh yeah. There was plenty. Might even have some left over for Christmas. HILBERT Ah. Very good. ... You did not happen to find any pecan pie - ? EIFFEL Wait - so - there's - dinner is - you just...? There's - (growl) There's real turkey?! (must be dreaming) There's real turkey? (so excited) There's - ? Processing his change of fortune, Eiffel makes some HIGH- PITCHED, SPUTTERING NOISES. MINKOWSKI You okay, Eiffel? EIFFEL I - I - ... Yeah. Honestly, right now I don't know whether to kiss you or punch you, Commander. HILBERT Either one would get you immediately jettisoned out of #3 Airlock. MINKOWSKI That is true. EIFFEL But w-w-w-w-wait. If you were making dinner this entire time, why did you put me on cooking duty? And you sound a lot healthier than you did a while ago. 10. MINKOWSKI Oh, that was Hera's suggestion. She thought the best way to keep you away from the kitchen was to put you in charge of dinner. HILBERT Very sound hypothesis. MINKOWSKI Yeah. Although I do have some mixed feelings about her being right about that. EIFFEL Was

while ago. 10. MINKOWSKI Oh, that was Hera's suggestion. She thought the best way to keep you away from the kitchen was to put you in charge of dinner. HILBERT Very sound hypothesis. MINKOWSKI Yeah. Although I do have some mixed feelings about her being right about that. EIFFEL Was everybody in on this?! HILBERT I was not. MINKOWSKI That is also true. HILBERT I do not care. MINKOWSKI And that is also true. Doesn't mean you get to skip dinner, Doctor. HILBERT Mmm. EIFFEL Okay, fine. But if we could go back to what's really important here? (so excited) OhmyGod ohmyGod, we have real turkey! Minkowski CHUCKLES. MINKOWSKI C'mon. You can thank me when we all sit down at the table and go around one by one. EIFFEL Oh, it'll be my pleasure! Lead the way, Commander... Lead the way. On that, we - FADE OUT. 11. END OF EPISODE.

WOLF 359 "HAPPY HOLIDAYS" by Michelle Agresti NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. Mission Mishaps, Entry Eight. SI-5 Surveillance Log, Entry CRT004. We hear a DOOR OPEN. KEPLER Mr. Jacobi! Would you do me the favor of stepping into my office for a moment? If you would be so kind? JACOBI Uhh... just a moment, sir. I’m – I’m in the middle of, um... uh, some very sensitive work - ? Kepler GROWLS. JACOBI (CONT'D) ... which I would love to put on hold to step into your office right this instant. Yes, sir. Of course. He STEPS towards Kepler's office. KEPLER Good. You too, Doctor. MAXWELL Uh, but, sir, I - KEPLER Office. Now. MAXWELL Yes, of course, sir. I would be delighted to. Maxwell follows and catches up to Jacobi. MAXWELL (CONT'D) (whisper) Why are we in trouble? Did you do something again? JACOBI (whisper) I dunno. The door SLAMS SHUT. 2. KEPLER Thank you for joining me here. Please, have a seat. Jacobi and Maxwell SIT DOWN. KEPLER (CONT'D) Mr. Jacobi. Would you please tell me what this is? Kepler slides a piece of paper over to Jacobi. JACOBI Do you mean like in a broader, more philosophical sense, or - Kepler CLEARS HIS THROAT. JACOBI (CONT'D) Uh, I mean, it's - well, it looks like a progress report on all of our active missions. KEPLER Very good. Very good. Doctor Maxwell: how would you characterize the progress of your work to date? MAXWELL Oh, ah - well, you could - I mean, there's all kinds of ways that you could answer that question - KEPLER In a word, please. How's it going? MAXWELL Oh, ah... I'd say... great? KEPLER "Great"? MAXWELL Yes! Just... great. It's going great. KEPLER Mr. Jacobi, would we agree with that characterization? Does that sound right to you? 3. JACOBI Yeah? ... Yeah! I think that great's a pretty, uh, great word for where we're at. KEPLER You think so? JACOBI Yes? I - I mean, the reports are all in and uh, prep is going well for the next week's deployment, so as far as I can see, everything is perfectly - Kepler presents him with another piece of paper. JACOBI (CONT'D) Ah. There's that. KEPLER Mhm. Eloquent as always, Jacobi. Why did I ever doubt that things would be "perfectly, ah, there's that"? Whatever it was

the next week's deployment, so as far as I can see, everything is perfectly - Kepler presents him with another piece of paper. JACOBI (CONT'D) Ah. There's that. KEPLER Mhm. Eloquent as always, Jacobi. Why did I ever doubt that things would be "perfectly, ah, there's that"? Whatever it was that made Jacobi react that way - Kepler shows it to Maxwell, too. KEPLER (CONT'D) Maxwell? MAXWELL Oh. Ah - um - well, sir - it's possible that I overstated the progress on the... KEPLER Greatly. Overstated? MAXWELL I - I'm, uh, I'm just - I'm just saying that... Maxwell's phone BUZZES. MAXWELL (CONT'D) (unconvincing lie) Oh! Oh God! It's a message... from... it - it looks like the Quasar data processor, uh - it's - it's developed a binary fault in its cortical network! 4. JACOBI Oh no! Not a binary fault in the quanticoritcuhh - Uhh. That's bad! MAXWELL Yes! Yes it is! I need to go deal with this. Right now. JACOBI Do you need help with that emergency? MAXWELL Oh that's very nice of you, but I should be just fi- Jacobi ELBOWS her. MAXWELL (CONT'D) Mm! (under her breath) Why you lousy - (exhales) On second thought. Yes! I'd love some help, Jacobi. JACOBI I thought you might. MAXWELL Someone needs to... shut off the left particle beam from... inside the radiation chamber. JACOBI Sounds delish! We should get on it right away. Yes? MAXWELL Yes. They both stand to leave. MAXWELL (CONT'D) We should. Ah, so, excuse us, Major, but we're just gonna go deal with this. Super quickly... JACOBI Like, so fast you'll barely know we've gone. 5. MAXWELL Although, now that I think about it, it is a pretty tricky process, so it could take a couple of hours. JACOBI Or days! MAXWELL Days! Yes. Definitely a possibility. So don't wait for us or anything, Major. JACOBI How about we, um, we call you when we get done with it, and - MAXWELL Yes! Exactly. Uh, you should just do whatever you were going to do without the two of - Kepler CLAPS. Slowly. MAXWELL (CONT'D) But with - without the two of us - what... why is there clapping? What - what does the clapping - JACOBI It means it didn't work, Maxwell. MAXWELL Dammit. KEPLER Have a seat. Maxwell and Jacobi both SIGH and sit back down. KEPLER (CONT'D) Both

CLAPS. Slowly. MAXWELL (CONT'D) But with - without the two of us - what... why is there clapping? What - what does the clapping - JACOBI It means it didn't work, Maxwell. MAXWELL Dammit. KEPLER Have a seat. Maxwell and Jacobi both SIGH and sit back down. KEPLER (CONT'D) Both of you are better operatives than you are actors. Just out of curiosity, what was that alert actually about, Doctor? MAXWELL The Chinese food I ordered is waiting for me downstairs. KEPLER Mm, Chinese food... I hope it goes without saying that I am furious, and this is unacceptable. 6. MAXWELL Yes, sir, but - KEPLER No, no, no, you know the rules. There will be no "buts" after a "yes, sir." You know that. MAXWELL Yes, sir. JACOBI Yes, sir. KEPLER Better. Now, what has gotten into the two of you? Are you that desperate to weasel your way out of tonight? MAXWELL Yes. JACOBI Like, completely desperate! MAXWELL Desperately desperate! KEPLER That was rhetorical. Also: tough! You both knew this job would require some unusual assignments. JACOBI Yeah, but not like this! This is different. KEPLER Why? JACOBI Because I don't do holiday parties! KEPLER (slow laughter) Oh, yes, you do, Mr. Jacobi. Yes, you do... Kepler waves a piece of paper in front of him. KEPLER (CONT'D) ... No matter what your hilariously misinformed RSVP says to the contrary. 7. MAXWELL But, sir! Uh, look, to be perfectly frank, I could really use that extra time - to work on my - KEPLER But, Doctor! I thought all your active missions were going... great. Maxwell SIGHS. MAXWELL Yes. Yes, I did say that. KEPLER And did you lie? MAXWELL No, sir. KEPLER Ergo? Happy holidays! JACOBI Uh, what about my excuse? KEPLER What was your excuse? JACOBI That I don't do holiday parties? KEPLER Exactly. I did not dignify it with a response. Jacobi sinks into his seat. KEPLER (CONT'D) You are both attending this holiday party. Do you need me to say "mandatory" again? MAXWELL But why is it so important that we all - KEPLER Ooh. Careful, Maxwell. What are the first three rules of the SI-5 handbook? Maxwell SIGHS. 8. MAXWELL "Don't ask any unnecessary questions." KEPLER Mhmm. MAXWELL "Don't ask any frivolous questions." KEPLER Very good. And? MAXWELL "Don't ask any questions." KEPLER Precisely. This is Goddard Futuristics. You do not ask why.

Ooh. Careful, Maxwell. What are the first three rules of the SI-5 handbook? Maxwell SIGHS. 8. MAXWELL "Don't ask any unnecessary questions." KEPLER Mhmm. MAXWELL "Don't ask any frivolous questions." KEPLER Very good. And? MAXWELL "Don't ask any questions." KEPLER Precisely. This is Goddard Futuristics. You do not ask why. You trust that you were given orders for a reason. You attend the holiday party. I will see both of you with eggnog and gingerbread cookies at 2100 hours. Do not disappoint me. Anything else? Jacobi raises his hand. KEPLER (CONT'D) Jacobi? JACOBI What if we - hypothetically - didn't have anything to wear to this occasion? MAXWELL Oh! Me too! Ah, I might - I might also be in that hypothetical situation. KEPLER Well, I’m sure that two operatives of your caliber will have no trouble in acquiring appropriate outfits in the ample time - JACOBI Uh, two hours isn't exactly ample time. I mean, the - 9. KEPLER The very ample time before the start of the party. MAXWELL But, it - KEPLER Doctor? Beg it, borrow it, steal it, I don't care. Just find something, and get there before Cutter starts the Secret Santa exchange. Now, if there isn't anything else - MAXWELL My mother just died! BEAT. KEPLER Excuse me? MAXWELL Ah, my... very dear mother just... died. Tragically. KEPLER Maxwell. We all know that you don't talk to your mother. JACOBI My mother just died? KEPLER No, Jacobi. MAXWELL I'm sick! JACOBI I caught it from her! KEPLER Enough. This discussion is over. I will see you at 2100, if I do not hear you both caroling first. JACOBI But - KEPLER You are going to be festive... 10. MAXWELL Sir! KEPLER ... and merry, and we are all going to have a wonderful, jolly time! Right? MAXWELL Yes, sir. JACOBI Yes, sir. KEPLER Excellent. Now. Get the hell out of my office, and go back to work. Jacobi and Maxwell start to leave. The door OPENS. KEPLER (CONT'D) Oh, and Jacobi? Maxwell? From the leadership at Goddard Futuristics: Happy Holidays. FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.
EPISODE.

WOLF 359 "LIGHTS OUT" by Gabriel Urbina NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. Mission Mishaps, Entry Five. Hephaestus Log WYL320. Day 487. We hear the sound of PAGES being turned. EIFFEL (miles away) This is the log of Communications Officer Doug Eiffel... on board the... uhh... on board the... Officer Eiffel on the... (snorts) Oh man. (clears his throat) Sorry, this is uh - this is Officer Eiffel, Hephaestus, Day 487, and it's Log O'Clock, yadda yadda yadda. Sorry if I sound a little distracted, listeners. We, uh... found something today. He runs his thumb over the pages of a book. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Minkowski fished this out of one of the old storage compartments. H.P. Lovecraft. Call of Cthu- (clears his throat) Call of Cthu- Cthul- C...thu- ... Chuck. The Call of Chuck and Other Weird Stories. And some of these stories, man? Listen to this: He leafs through the book, settling on - EIFFEL (CONT'D) (campfire stories) "There was a nightmare flight through a vast cobwebbed nave whose ghostly arches reached up to realms of leering shadows, a sightless scramble through a littered basement, a climb to regions of air and street lights outside, and a mad racing down a spectral hill of gibbering gables"... (normal voice) Not bad, eh? I mean, it's no R.L. Stine, but at least it's better than Minkowski's manuals. Which I've totally read. Totally. All the words. Read 'em! Yup. 2. He flips through some more pages. EIFFEL (CONT'D) No idea why this is up here, though. It looks pretty darn beat up, if you ask me, and - well, you'd think the entertainment budget wouldn't cover - Something catches his eye. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Oh, wait a minute. There's an inscription at the end here. Kinda hard to make out. "Property of Dr. K-" There's a FLARE from the star. The LIGHTS FLICKER. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ohhh. For God's...! Yeah, that's the other thing. Star's something- or-other is flaring up, so we gotta be in low power mode until it's done blowing off steam. Apparently these flare things might scramble Hera's brains or something, so she's offline until the weather improves. Which means we have to make do without hot water, AC, microwaves, whatever she does to keep the aft deck from smelling like a - AH! Another FLARE. He WINCES. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Man... They also make the light from the

brains or something, so she's offline until the weather improves. Which means we have to make do without hot water, AC, microwaves, whatever she does to keep the aft deck from smelling like a - AH! Another FLARE. He WINCES. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Man... They also make the light from the star get way brighter. It's just like a... a flash for a second, everything's red and... FLARES and DISTANT RUMBLING. Not a promising sound. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Uhh... hm. One second. Lemme make sure this is still in the lands of "what an annoying minor inconvenience" and not in other more... "oh God oh God" places. COMMS BUZZER. 3. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Hey, Doc? Are these flare things still more or less under control? Do I need to be concerned? HILBERT (over comms) Not now, Eiffel. In middle of delicate situation. Besides, should you not be focused on archiving Minkowski's findings from storage unit? EIFFEL Y...es. I am... one hundred percent working on that. Got the filing cabinet right here. Eiffel OPENS and CLOSES a drawer of the filing cabinet to demonstrate. EIFFEL (CONT'D) But, um. What about you though? Everything okay? HILBERT Perfectly satisfactory, Eiffel. Or at least it will be, once I locate problem element. EIFFEL ... Alright. Let's try that again. Doc... use your words. What's. The. Problem? HILBERT Problem is not... (frustrated sound) Fine. If you must know. One of my more volatile samples from my laboratory has - SNAP. The power SHUTS OFF. EIFFEL What the-? ... (to himself; muttering) Oh, great. I guess the storm knocked out our power generator. As if things weren't bad enough, now I gotta sit in the dark? Man... (to Hilbert) Anyway. Sorry, Doc. What was that? (MORE) 4. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (beat) Doc? You there? He presses the button on the comms panel a few times. Line's dead. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Aaand the comms are out too! Awesome! FLARES. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Yeah, yeah, yeah. You showed us who's boss. Don't act so pleased with yourself. (beat) Well, folks. Not that listening to me sitting by myself in the dark not being able to read my new book isn't riveting, but I think I'll spare you the excitement. From Wolf 359, this has been the log of Communications Officer Doug- He cuts himself off as he notices a sound - something SKITTERING in the dark. EIFFEL (CONT'D) What the-? ... I'm sorry. F-for

to read my new book isn't riveting, but I think I'll spare you the excitement. From Wolf 359, this has been the log of Communications Officer Doug- He cuts himself off as he notices a sound - something SKITTERING in the dark. EIFFEL (CONT'D) What the-? ... I'm sorry. F-for a moment I just thought I heard... Uhh... I don't even know, just... Nah, it's late. (clears his throat) This has been the log of Communications Offi- SKITTERING. Louder this time. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay. That time I - I'm pretty sure I heard something. ... Hello? Hellooo? Is anyone there? Commander? Hil...bert? ... What - what was it that Hilbert was saying about... one of the more volatile samples from the laboratory has... How did that sentence end? (getting more scared) The volatile sample has... what? Has... escaped from containment? Has... bitten off my arm and developed a taste for human flesh? Has... (MORE) 5. EIFFEL (CONT'D) successfully been turned into a deadly, deadly killing machine? Soft SKITTERING. CLICK. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Hey, Doc? Doc? You there? What were you saying? What was going on with your sample? No response. Eiffel lets out a scared, nervous laugh. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ooohhh-kay. This is, uh. This is... fine. Doug, you're just. You're just freaking yourself out for no reason. You just stayed up too late reading some horror stories and now you're just imagining things. But there's absolutely no reason for you to - AH! He YELPS at the sudden FLARE. And in that flash of light... EIFFEL (CONT'D) What the - ? ... Oh... my God. Oh... my... God... okay. I definitely just saw it. There's - there's something in this room with me. In the flash from the flare, I saw it. Oh God. What is it? It's small... and scurrying... and... SKITTERING. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Aaahh... it's moving around... it's - it's getting closer, isn't it? FLARE. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Where is it? Where is it? Where did it go? A terrified BEAT as his eyes dart around the dark room. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay. Okay. Maybe it just... went away. (MORE) 6. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Maybe it didn't think I looked all that delic- interesting ... and so it went away. Yeah. That's probably what- FLARE. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (even quieter; even more panicked) Oh God. No, it didn't go away. It's right between me and the door. And it's just... sitting there... staring

EIFFEL (CONT'D) Maybe it didn't think I looked all that delic- interesting ... and so it went away. Yeah. That's probably what- FLARE. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (even quieter; even more panicked) Oh God. No, it didn't go away. It's right between me and the door. And it's just... sitting there... staring at me. Can it see in the dark? Oh God, what if it can see in the- FLARE. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ahh! It's getting closer! I - I need to get out of here, I - FLARE. EIFFEL (CONT'D) No. It's right on top of me... I gotta... I've gotta do some... FLARE. EIFFEL (CONT'D) AHHH! Eat file cabinet, you unholy science monster! CLANK. CRASH. He tips the filing cabinet over. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Did - did I actually...? (relief giving way to victory) Oh, yeah! That's right! Die, you grotesque bastard! And when you're down in Hell, tell all your buddies not to mess with Communications Officer Doug Ei- The door OPENS. A flashlight CLICKS on. HILBERT Eiffel? What are you doing? 7. EIFFEL Ha ha. Hey, Doc. Oh, I just did you one heck of a solid. HILBERT You did... solid? EIFFEL Yup! I just made your little sample problem a lot less volatile, if ya know what I mean. You're welcome. HILBERT My... sample? EIFFEL Yep! HILBERT The... aluminium compound sample that is currently oxidizing in laboratory? EIFFEL Yep, that's the - ... Aluminum? Soo... not an escaped mutant monster? HILBERT Definitely not. EIFFEL Then... what did I just splat with the...? He tips the filing cabinet back up and off of it. Hilbert shines the flashlight on the squashed... thing. HILBERT Hm... Well. That looks like - Yes. Is repair and maintenance drone. EIFFEL Maintenance drone? Wait, we have - we have a maintenance drone? HILBERT Who did you think calibrated systems? Magic pixies? EIFFEL (sputtering) But - I - But - I - I didn't know we have maintenance drones! 8. HILBERT We do not have drones. We have drone. Singular. The drone SPARKS and POWERS DOWN. HILBERT (CONT'D) Well. We had drone. Now we have... something different. EIFFEL And... what's that? HILBERT More work, Officer Eiffel. More work. Well done. EIFFEL Ohhhh.... And as Eiffel whines, we - FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.
different. EIFFEL And... what's that? HILBERT More work, Officer Eiffel. More work. Well done. EIFFEL Ohhhh.... And as Eiffel whines, we - FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

WOLF 359 "NO COMPLAINTS" by Zach Libresco and Noah Masur NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. Mission Mishaps, Entry Seven. SI-5 Surveillance Log Entry FRD916. We hear the sounds of RAIN and WINDSHIELD WIPERS. Kepler is sitting in a car. After a moment, the door opens, and Jacobi slumps in, soaking wet. Jacobi SNIFFS, and then SIGHS. JACOBI I. Hate. Stakeouts. KEPLER Rule number eight. JACOBI What? KEPLER Rule number eight. Of the SI-5 handbook? “All operatives shall display high levels of morale, enthusiasm, zeal, zest, and/or vigor throughout all active fieldwork operations." JACOBI So what you're telling me is...? KEPLER No. Complaints. JACOBI What is that asinine, micromanaging tactic supposed to a- Kepler CLEARS HIS THROAT. JACOBI (CONT'D) Uhh, I mean, aside from being... super... mega... great. KEPLER Better. BEAT. Jacobi SHIVERS. JACOBI Goddamn rain. I'm freezing. KEPLER What did I just say? 2. JACOBI Yeah. Sorry. Sorry. I guess I must've just forgotten it. KEPLER Pass me those binoculars? JACOBI Yeah, sure. Jacobi passes Kepler the binoculars. BEAT. Jacobi hums to himself. JACOBI (CONT'D) How about some snacks? Who wants snacks? "Ooh, ooh, Jacobi! I want some!" Heh, well, you got it, Jacobi. Let me get those for you. He POPS open a can of Pringles. KEPLER Keep it down. JACOBI Yeah, yeah, yeah. CRUNCH CRUNCH CRUNCH. KEPLER They have anything good? JACOBI They didn't have crap. (under his breath) Stupid store. Useless town - KEPLER Focus. We are on a mission. JACOBI Yeah, which... why the hell are we even doing this? KEPLER We've got a candidate. For SI-5 work. So we're - JACOBI Stalking them. 3. KEPLER Vetting them, Jacobi. Vetting. CRUNCH CRUNCH CRUNCH. JACOBI (mouth full) What's so special about this candidate? KEPLER What do you mean? JACOBI Why all the special vetting? (beat) Ohh my... You do this for every candidate. KEPLER Well, not me personally. JACOBI So anyone that Cutter sends your way, you just follow them around. For days. KEPLER We only work with the best. We need to know they are the best. JACOBI Oh, Jesus. Did you - did you vet me? Before you recruited me? KEPLER What, you think we just happened to bump into each other? Please. I knew everything about you. JACOBI Yeah. I guess you did know everything about me. ... You know what day it is today? KEPLER Yes. I do.

you - did you vet me? Before you recruited me? KEPLER What, you think we just happened to bump into each other? Please. I knew everything about you. JACOBI Yeah. I guess you did know everything about me. ... You know what day it is today? KEPLER Yes. I do. An expectant BEAT. JACOBI You're really gonna make me say it. What day is it today, Major? 4. KEPLER Today... is Tuesday. BEAT. JACOBI I really hate you sometimes. KEPLER Hey. Rule number eight. JACOBI Ugh. (upbeat) I really hate you sometimes, sir! You're the worst! (normal voice) It's the 24th, by the way. BEAT. KEPLER (off Jacobi's look) What? JACOBI It's - nah. It's nothing. Forget it. KEPLER What's going on, Jacobi? JACOBI Nothing. KEPLER Is this about the duck thing? JACOBI It's not abou- No, it's not about the duck thing! KEPLER Because you know I'm - JACOBI No, stop it! KEPLER Excuse me? JACOBI It's not worth - 5. KEPLER I give the orders around here. JACOBI Yeah. I got that. BEAT. KEPLER For the record, I am sorry. JACOBI It's fine, sir. KEPLER If I'd known you'd cared that much, I wouldn't have done it. JACOBI Yes, sir. KEPLER Or sent the video to everyone in the - JACOBI Sir. It's no big deal. We can leave it alone. (beat) Well, what happened in the world today? Where's that newspaper? The seat CREAKS as he turns around to look for it. KEPLER Hey, cut it out! We have to watch for movement. JACOBI I can't even read the paper? KEPLER No! What if we miss our target? What if we're ambushed? JACOBI By whom? KEPLER Put it away, Jacobi. Do as you are told. 6. JACOBI Fine. (pointed) I'm sure nothing important happened today anyway. BEAT. KEPLER Alright, fine, you want something to pass the time? JACOBI Oh God, yes. Anything. KEPLER Why don't we play a game? JACOBI Except that. KEPLER How about Questions Only? JACOBI Especially not that. I should've never taught you that stupid game. KEPLER You don't think it's fun? JACOBI I used to. KEPLER You used to think it was fun? JACOBI What else could I have possibly meant by that? KEPLER Maybe you were looking for something else to occupy your attention? JACOBI If I go along with this, can I at least read the paper? KEPLER I don't know,

used to. KEPLER You used to think it was fun? JACOBI What else could I have possibly meant by that? KEPLER Maybe you were looking for something else to occupy your attention? JACOBI If I go along with this, can I at least read the paper? KEPLER I don't know, can you? 7. JACOBI (groan) May I? KEPLER Would reading the paper improve the mission's probability of success? JACOBI Is the mission's success the only thing you care about? KEPLER Is something bothering you? JACOBI I don't know, is it? KEPLER Wouldn't we say that answer isn't up to the standard we would expect from Questions Only? JACOBI Yeah, well. Rule number eight: no complaints. (beat) Okay, what if, hypothetically speaking, you had a friend. Or a colleague. And you thought there was an important day in your relationship. And it's a big landmark in your life, and, sure, maybe you should've communicated that it was important to you, but maybe your... partner... should've just known. But after all of that, your friend showed no signs of remembering your special friend day. Wouldn't you feel a bit unappreciated? BEAT. KEPLER Did I try communicating explicitly with this hypothetical person? JACOBI Yes. KEPLER Was that a question? 8. JACOBI No. (sigh) How much longer? KEPLER However long it takes to get a visual on the target. Or, if necessary, an intercept. Jacobi GROANS. KEPLER (CONT'D) I'd say no more than six hours. Jacobi GROANS louder. BEAT. KEPLER (CONT'D) Oh! JACOBI Yes? KEPLER Ohhh! I see... JACOBI What? KEPLER It was on your mind... JACOBI What are you talking about? KEPLER You know, Young thought it might be the case! JACOBI What case? KEPLER I told her no! But she insisted. JACOBI About what? KEPLER That is today. Tuesday. 9. JACOBI The 24th. KEPLER The day I recruited you. One year ago. JACOBI Today! (sarcastic) Oh. Oh, is that today? Is it? Huh, I hadn't noticed. KEPLER That is kind of special. JACOBI Yeah, no kidding. ... Well. I'm glad you finally remembered. KEPLER We should do something to mark this occasion! JACOBI If you say “Sit in this car in silence for the next ten hours”, I will scream. KEPLER Will you open that duffel bag? JACOBI Duffel... bag...? KEPLER Backseat? Jacobi UNZIPS the duffel bag. JACOBI Uhh... There's about fifty pounds of fireworks back here. KEPLER Yeah. I thought there

JACOBI If you say “Sit in this car in silence for the next ten hours”, I will scream. KEPLER Will you open that duffel bag? JACOBI Duffel... bag...? KEPLER Backseat? Jacobi UNZIPS the duffel bag. JACOBI Uhh... There's about fifty pounds of fireworks back here. KEPLER Yeah. I thought there might be! JACOBI What's - what's this for? KEPLER For tonight's celebration? 10. JACOBI What? KEPLER Yeah. Wanna set them off? JACOBI But... what about the stakeout? Shouldn't we be trying to keep a low profile? KEPLER Nah, our target’s not even home. We did the recruitment operation yesterday. JACOBI ... Are you... serious? So. All of this was... what? A prank? You pranked me? KEPLER Hey! Rule number eight. JACOBI Yeah, I know, I know, I'm not complaining! (softer) I'm not complaining. And on that we - FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

episode_id: Mission Mishaps - The Space You_re In
text: WOLF 359 "THE SPACE YOU'RE IN" by Sarah Shachat NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. Mission Mishaps, Entry Two. Hephaestus Log KWE824. Day 202. INT. HEPHAESTUS STATION - COMMS ROOM STATIC EIFFEL (flat) This is the audio log of Communications Officer Doug Eiffel on board the U.S.S. Hephaestus Station, everything is great, everyone is awesome, I'm doing my job super-er hard, and it's been great catching up, so let's do this again soon - Before he can get that last word out, we can hear something being PRINTED. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Hm. What's going on here? He TEARS the printed message off. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Alright, let's see... A-hem: "Message to Communications Officer Eiffel from Lieutenant Commander Minkowski: Eiffel, remember we agreed that you would start logging your mandatory hours of spacewalking." (scoffs) "You're in fact overdue for starting to log your hours. So get to it. That's an order." Ugh. Yeah, right. If the Minkowski thinks I'm gonna actually risk my life just for the regs, she's got another th- PRINTING. TEAR. EIFFEL "P.S. Please stop going off on weird tangents during your logs. (MORE) 2. EIFFEL (CONT'D) It's starting to sound a bit unprofessional." (beat) Well. In for a penny in for a pound, am I right, Canaveral? Besides, I'm not sure if anyone even listens to these things. If my voice is reaching anyone from Planet Intern: Greetings! (alien voice) We come in peace. (normal voice; chuckling at his own joke) But right now? Let's get to it! Specifically, let's get to Operation Hide. And Just Keep On Hiding. (scoffs) Oh, don't give me that imaginary look, Command Intern! I'm guessing you've seen 2001, you know how this goes! First it's all "no exceptions, rules are rules" and then some hapless idiot ends up hurled into the cold abyss! Nooo thank you! Until spacewalking is statistically the safest way to travel, I know where I'm hanging out, and it's with my two best buds: heat and air. Which means today we're going on an adventure! A secret adventure, to a spot where Herr Kommandant would never look for - He SNAPS his fingers. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Oh! I've got it. INT. HEPHAESTUS STATION - BRIDGE We hear a DOOR OPEN. EIFFEL Okay, Command Intern, welcome to the bridge. Using Clark Kent logic, I think I can probably camp out here and avoid Minkowski- MINKOWSKI Eiffel? That

Didn't think you'd be coming up here any time soon. MINKOWSKI I'm in charge of the station's navigation functions, Eiffel. I spend most of my day here. ... Why are you up here, though? Shouldn't you be - EIFFEL I'm here for work! Uh, for work reasons, that's why I'm here! MINKOWSKI Oh. Are you finally getting around to updating our star charts? BEAT. EIFFEL Yes. MINKOWSKI (explaining to a child) Well, you're going to want to use terminal two for that. It has all our relevant positioning data. EIFFEL That sounds amazing, Commander, really great, so so stoked for those star charts, I uh - I just, uh - realized I forgot something! MINKOWSKI Okay? EIFFEL Soo... I'm gonna go get that thing! That I forgot! Then I'll be... right back! INT. HEPHAESTUS STATION - HILBERT'S LAB Eiffel tumbles in, out of breath, and SLAMS THE DOOR shut behind him. 4. EIFFEL (panting) Phew! Okay. Okay, that was a close one. I think I'm in the clear, though. I know I'm not supposed to be in Hilbert's lab, but maybe as long as I don't break any of the fancy equip- He bumps into something. SLAM. Something made of glass falls and SHATTERS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Woah! Okay! Um. Nevermind. Maybe I should - HILBERT Ah! Officer Eiffel. Excellent timing. EIFFEL Yep. Uh huh. Definitely. I am here intentionally to do something for you, Doctor. HILBERT Good. As previously discussed, I require you to lay greenhouse foundation so that I may begin specimen incubation period. Should not take more than sixteen to eighteen hours. EIFFEL Uhh... HILBERT And you will have to be very careful about inhaling the fertilizer. It may cause temporary blindness. No, wait. It will cause blindness. Blindness may be temporary. EIFFEL Uh, Doc, that sounds awesome, I - I'm very excited to get started Johnny Appleseeding all of that, but, uh, y'know, I just gotta check in on something in the comms room first? HILBERT Is it absolutely necessary - 5. EIFFEL Yes, yes it is! See

HILBERT Is it absolutely necessary - 5. EIFFEL Yes, yes it is! See you later, byeee! With that, Eiffel is already out the door. INT. HEPHAESTUS STATION - ARMORY Eiffel SIGHS. EIFFEL Okay. Okay. There's literally no way anyone's gonna come in here. I'm not even sure why we have an armory. I mean, we're not exactly the Three Amigos, but no one's got a reason to Rambo it up either. He opens one of the storage lockers. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Jeez, look at all these things. He starts picking things up and rummaging around. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Um, Command Intern? There's a harpoon on our scientific research station. Why Call Me Ishmael Futuristics put a harpoon on our scientific research station, I do not quite under- COMMS BUZZER. MINKOWSKI Eiffel. Hera says that you're in the armory? EIFFEL Uhh... MINKOWSKI Let's try this again. Officer Eiffel, what are you doing in the armory? EIFFEL W-well, you see... Commander... I'm... I feel bad. I know that there's a lot of stuff I've been neglecting, and so... I'm finally... getting around to- 6. MINKOWSKI Cross-referencing our small arms ammunition with the allotment in the manifest? EIFFEL That's the one! Yep! Minkowski SIGHS. MINKOWSKI Well, be quick about it, okay? I still need you to update those star charts. TRANSITIONAL WHIP. EIFFEL Okay, there's no way in hell anyone would ever look for me in- BUZZER. HILBERT Eiffel, if you happen to be in observation deck, there is a series of very simple adjustments I would like you to make to viewing canopy. Is simple procedure that will only take one, or two, or three, or four hours... Eiffel GROANS in agony. WHIP. EIFFEL Okay, Command Intern, no one has any reason to be down in - BUZZER. MINKOWSKI Eiffel, if you're down in engineering, could you check the temperature controls? I want to make sure the engines are running in the optimal environment. EIFFEL (to himself) Oh, come on! WHIP. 7. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (whispering) Just be quiet. Just be very, very... BUZZER. MINKOWSKI Eiffel, I don't know why you're in filtration, but since you're there, double check that the emergency backup system has enough coolant. BUZZER.

HILBERT Officer Eiffel, what is progress on pH level tests? ... Officer Eiffel? Are you there? BUZZER. MINKOWSKI Eiffel! Do you read me? Eiffel WHIMPERS. BUZZER. HILBERT There is much to do, Officer Eiffel. Respond. Eiffel kind of SQUEAKS. BUZZER. MINKOWSKI You can't avoid answering forever, Eiffel. There's only so much space on this station. EIFFEL (under his breath) There's only... so much... (light bulb) That's it! WHIP. We can tell from the change in ambient sound that we're now - 8. EXT. U.S.S. HEPHAESTUS STATION Eiffel, spacewalking. EIFFEL That's better. Ahh. You know, I don't know what I was so afraid about, being out here. There's so much to love... the peace. The simplicity. All you are is the space you're in, and that space is connected to... all the space. It's beautiful, really. No petty demands, no annoying chores, no murderously pissed off superior officers who realized you've been avoiding them... COMMS STATIC. MINKOWSKI Eiffel, get back in here right the hell now. EIFFEL Ah, sorry, Commander, got my mandatory spacewalk time to do... MINKOWSKI Eiffel, you cretinous lizard. There are dozens of higher priority tasks ahead of your stupid EVA Hours. And besides, I'm telling you to abort and come back in here. Now. EIFFEL So are you saying that you're going back on your official orders? Are you going to make an exception for- MINKOWSKI I am saying that if you continue - EIFFEL Because it's really important that crew members follow the rules and regulations... MINKOWSKI Oh-kay. Eiffel. You have, what? Twelve hours on that tank? EIFFEL Uhh... little less, actually? 9. MINKOWSKI Okay then. Enjoy every minute. Because, after they're over, you're gonna have to come back inside. And when you do, I will be happy to show you how much of an exception I am willing to make for you. Specifically, to the limits of corporal punishment. EIFFEL Hang on, Commander, I only - STATIC. Eiffel SIGHS. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (whine) I hate space. And on that we - FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

WOLF 359 "THE VELDT" by Sarah Shachat NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. Mission Mishaps, Entry Four. Hephaestus Log NOL858. Day 411. INT. HEPHAESTUS STATION - GREENHOUSE DOOR OPENS. We can hear the sound of a MISTING SYSTEM. EIFFEL Whoa. Hellooo? Helloooo? His voice ECHOES. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Man, this whole greenhouse thing is... kind of an understatement. More like a Green McMansion. COMMS BUZZER. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay, Doc. Present and accounted for. Operation Willy Wonka and the O2 Factory is a go. HILBERT (over comms) I am going to assume that means you've arrived at the greenhouse. EIFFEL Which, remind me again why I- HILBERT You are there to regulate irrigation channels with volumetric flow meter. EIFFEL So basically earning my allowance money by mowing the grass? HILBERT There is no gr- Mmmf. Nevermind. Service nodes are placed at regular intervals. Follow instructions in manual. Take measurements. EIFFEL Yeah, yeah, yeah, don't worry, Doc. I'll catch 'em all. 2. HILBERT What? Catch... who? EIFFEL Cause I wanna be the very best? Like no one ever was? It's a... nevermind. Go back to whatever ultra-science you're cooking up in the lab. HILBERT Very... very well. COMMS BUZZER. EIFFEL (Australian accent) G'day, explorer mavericks! This is your buckaroo pal and guide to adventure, Cap'n D.F. Eiffel! You're in for a treat today, listener chums, 'cause we're going into the Veldt. We're entering a wild place, where nature, and man, and Goddard Futuristics proprietary technology all lock horns in a beautiful dance that weaves together the fabrics of - (clears throat)(normal voice) This is where we use a bunch of plants to make our air. (Australian accent) Now as any explorer can tell you, air is important. In space, nobody can hear you scream, and since working on this rustbucket involves a lot of screaming, we've got to make quite a lot of air. (normal voice) And that air has to be A1A top of the line no defects particulars or funny smells allowed, the highest quality air this side of the Leo Constell- OW! He just HIT HIS HEAD against something metal. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ugh, stupid... okay. I think I found node number one. Jeez, this looks... complicated. Maybe, uh... this one? 3. He FLIPS A SWITCH. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Um... A screen powers up. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay? Screen's loading. ... "Please stand by for start up instructions. Failure

HEAD against something metal. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ugh, stupid... okay. I think I found node number one. Jeez, this looks... complicated. Maybe, uh... this one? 3. He FLIPS A SWITCH. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Um... A screen powers up. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Okay? Screen's loading. ... "Please stand by for start up instructions. Failure to properly execute start up will result in an immediate reset"? Ugh. Way to put pressure on me, pal. TEXT fills the screen. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Alrighty. "Step one: input red password." Red password? What the hell is the red - Oh. Manual. Hilbert did say... Right. Let's see. Uhh... He FLIPS through the manual. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Got it! As he types the password in: EIFFEL (CONT'D) Red password: Alpha-Whiskey-Kilo- Sierra-Juliet-Echo-9. ERROR. EIFFEL (CONT'D) The hell? TEXT. EIFFEL (CONT'D) "Please stand by for start up instructions. Failure to properly execute instructions"... wow. You were not kidding. Did I not type in the red password? In all its... Oh, it's Oscar-9. Weird font. BEAT. 4. EIFFEL (CONT'D) (Australian accent) Uhh, hang on there a second while I get us on the road to adventure again, pilgrim travelers! INPUT. EIFFEL (CONT'D) What the... now it wants the blue password? Someone's needy. He FLIPS through the manual. EIFFEL (CONT'D) How many of these am I gonna- Ah- ha! Charlie-Foxtrot-Sierra-Sierra- Hotel-7-3. INPUT. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Alternate characters of red and blue passwords in reverse starting with red... Really? Wait, what? No, I know no one else is here, but really? What the hell? Why does this thing even need to be so secure, it's not like it controls our... (realizes what he's saying) Our air. ... Well. Okay then. (Australian accent) Alright, just one more second, trailblazer buckaroos, and then - adventure ho! On that note, we... CUT TO: Eiffel has been at this for a while now. We can imagine how exhausted he looks by the sound of his voice as he GROANS in frustration. HILBERT (over comms) And you're sure you pressed the side lever? 5. EIFFEL (frustrated) Yes, I pressed the stupid - oh wait, did you say the side lever? Hold on. CRANK. DING. HILBERT What is next? EIFFEL Next? There's a next?! TEXT. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Heh... EIFFEL And this thing wants that in binary...? HILBERT Correct. 6. EIFFEL Sooo...? HILBERT So you type 01000110... TYPING. EIFFEL Okay... HILBERT And again 01000110. TYPING. HILBERT (CONT'D) And again... EIFFEL Uh, hold on? ... What the-? Is that a timer? HILBERT You are taking too long. TYPING. TIMER TICKING. EIFFEL Why the hell is there a timer on the-? HILBERT Just type, Eiffel. EIFFEL I'm typing, I'm typing! ... Wait! Did you say three or four zeros? HILBERT Three! TYPING. EIFFEL Uhh... okay, okay! What's next? TIMER TICKING. (exhales) Say, Doc, isn't a little dirt in the air like, good for you or something -? HILBERT No, Eiffel. EIFFEL No, I'm serious! I - I read somewhere that it builds, uhh... character! HILBERT Should I inform the Commander that you are unwilling to perform basic station maintenance? EIFFEL No... HILBERT Well. Then you better type red password again. Eiffel WHIMPERS. 8. HILBERT (CONT'D) Come now, Officer Eiffel. Do not think of safety protocols as impediment. Think of them as... challenge. As adventure. EIFFEL (Australian accent) Hooray... Eiffel GRUMBLES at the task ahead of him and on that we - FADE OUT. END OF EPISODE.

text: WOLF 359 "YOU WANT, I SOLVE" by Zach Valenti NOTE: The recording script for this episode is not available. Mission Mishaps, Entry Three. Hephaestus Log WME389. Day 372. EIFFEL (quiet) This is the audio log of Communications Officer Doug Eiffel, reporting to you live, friends, on Day 372. Today's adventure: the Oh Dear God, I Really Hope Minkowski Doesn't Wake Up While I Ransack the Pantry for the Last Bag of Real Coffee caper. Now, I know what you're thinking: "Hold on, Officer Eiffel, won't getting up at 0500 to steal coffee just mean that you'll be more tired later in the day?" Well, to you, sir, I say this: sometimes it's not about being perky, alert, and ready to go, it's about the principle of the thing. Besides: (sings) "The best part of waking up is Folgers in your cup." SLURP. Eiffel GRIMACES. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Ugh. The cheap, cheap discount Folgers. Frickin' command. Er... I mean... I'm pretty sure that wasn't your department, whoever's listening to this, but... maybe... you could kick it up the ladder? Caffeine is serious business! And you guys are a business... of some sort, so you should be able to... Eiffel trails off. There's a STRANGE GROWL coming from behind him. He turns around, and can't believe what he's seeing. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Uhhh... hiii...? ... Hera, am I... seeing what I think I'm seeing...? (beat) Oh, right. She's still on her debugging spa day or whatever. 2. BARK. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Uh. Nice doggy? Nice... space doggy? On our animal-free station? Ohh, oh-kay, you definitely weren't in the manifest, buddy. So unless you've been hiding out in the walls - BARK BARK BARK. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Whoa, whoa! Easy, boy, easy! Before you wake everyone up. I have a feeling the mean lady who runs this place might not be super fond of you! He pets the dog. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Nobody needs to know you and I went on a Dunkin' Donuts run, huh, boy? Aww, you like that, don'tcha? Don'tcha? Aww, look at your adorable little... tails. Your adorable little tails. Oh boy. Um. You have two tails. (beat) Okay. Okay. This is new. Don't think I've ever seen your breed down at the pound. Has Hilbert gone really crazy, or...? BARK BARK. BARK BARK BARK. EIFFEL (CONT'D) Shh shh shh, come on, come on, come on, do me a solid and bring it down
